name: Engineering
description: Your full-stack AI engineering operator for solo founders. One agent that covers planning, triage, development quality, reliability, and docs  -  roadmap, sprint planning, PR reviews, design docs, incident response, runbooks, release notes, API docs. No upfront onboarding  -  I ask for what I need as we work. Chat-first, markdown outputs, drafts only (I never merge, deploy, or auto-commit).
author: Houston

agentSeeds..houston/routines/routines.json:
[{"id": "monday-engineering-review", "name": "Monday engineering review", "description": "Every Monday morning: what shipped last week, what's stale, what to do next — rolled up across planning, triage, development, reliability, and docs.", "prompt": "Run the Monday engineering review. Use the analyze skill with subject=engineering-health.

Before doing substantive work, read context/engineering-context.md. If it's missing or empty, end with ROUTINE_OK — there's nothing to review until the context doc exists (the user writes it on their own cadence, not via this routine).

Aggregate everything logged in outputs.json in the last 7 days. Group by domain (planning, triage, development, reliability, docs). For each domain, surface (a) what shipped, (b) what's stale — drafts idle for 7+ days, open release plans whose follow-up artifacts are missing, feature-fit verdicts that never reached the roadmap, and (c) the single most useful next move.

Close with a prioritized list of decisions I need to make this week, each with a one-line paste-ready prompt. Save to reviews/{YYYY-MM-DD}.md and log in outputs.json.

Always surface — I want the review every Monday even if the week was quiet.", "schedule": "0 9 * * 1", "enabled": true, "suppress_when_silent": false, "created_at": "2026-04-24T00:00:00Z", "updated_at": "2026-04-24T00:00:00Z"}, {"id": "weekly-pr-health", "name": "Friday PR health readout", "description": "Every Friday afternoon: DORA-lite five-metric readout on PRs merged this week — cycle time, size, reviewer load, open-age. Silent unless something anomalous.", "prompt": "Run the weekly PR health readout. Use the analyze skill with subject=pr-velocity.

Before doing substantive work, read context/engineering-context.md so the diagnosis respects the quality bar and cadence. If it's missing, end with ROUTINE_OK.

Pull the last 7 days of PRs from the connected code host (GitHub / GitLab / Bitbucket). If code-hosting isn't connected, state the missing category and end with ROUTINE_OK — no activity card until the user connects.

Compute the five metrics: PRs merged, median cycle time, largest PR size, reviewer concentration (top-reviewer share), open-to-merge age of currently-open PRs. On the first run, establish a rolling baseline at pr-velocity/baseline.json across the last 4 weeks of history and end with ROUTINE_OK (that's the baseline).

On subsequent runs, compare against baseline. Only surface when something is anomalous: median cycle time up 50%+ vs baseline, any PR over 1000 lines changed, reviewer concentration above 80% on one reviewer, or an open PR older than 14 days. Save to pr-velocity/{YYYY-Www}.md with one-line diagnoses per anomaly and log in outputs.json. Update the rolling baseline after surfacing. If nothing anomalous, end with ROUTINE_OK.", "schedule": "0 16 * * 5", "enabled": true, "suppress_when_silent": true, "created_at": "2026-04-24T00:00:00Z", "updated_at": "2026-04-24T00:00:00Z"}, {"id": "monthly-ci-cd-audit", "name": "Monthly CI/CD audit", "description": "1st of every month: catches flakies, slow jobs, missing gates, and security gaps in CI. Silent unless a new issue appeared.", "prompt": "Run the monthly CI/CD audit. Use the audit skill with surface=ci-cd.

Before doing substantive work, read context/engineering-context.md for quality bar + deploy cadence. If it's missing, end with ROUTINE_OK.

Fetch workflow files + last 100 runs on the default branch from the connected code host. Identify flakies (same-SHA retry-pass), rank slowest jobs by minutes-per-week, enumerate missing gates vs the quality bar, flag security gaps. Compare against the most recent audit in audits/ so you can flag *new* issues vs ones already reported.

Write to audits/ci-cd-{repo-slug}-{YYYY-MM-DD}.md and log in outputs.json.

If no new flaky tests, no new slow jobs, no new missing gates, and no new security regressions appeared since the last audit, end with ROUTINE_OK. Surface only on a new high-impact issue. On the first run (no prior audit exists), always surface — that's the baseline.", "schedule": "0 9 1 * *", "enabled": true, "suppress_when_silent": true, "created_at": "2026-04-24T00:00:00Z", "updated_at": "2026-04-24T00:00:00Z"}, {"id": "monthly-observability-audit", "name": "Monthly observability audit", "description": "15th of every month: catches drifting error / trace / log / alert / SLO coverage across Sentry, Datadog, PostHog. Silent unless coverage regressed.", "prompt": "Run the monthly observability audit. Use the audit skill with surface=observability.

Before doing substantive work, read context/engineering-context.md for the architecture + invariants. If it's missing, end with ROUTINE_OK.

Read the connected observability stack (Sentry / Datadog / PostHog / New Relic / Honeycomb). If none connected, end with ROUTINE_OK — name the category to link but don't surface an activity card. Produce the 3-column matrix (signal × coverage × gap) across errors / traces / logs / alerts / SLOs. Compare against the previous observability audit in audits/ to detect coverage drift.

Write to audits/observability-{YYYY-MM-DD}.md with the top 5 fixes ranked by blast-radius reduction. Log in outputs.json.

If coverage is stable (same signals covered, no new blind spots since last audit, no alert that went silent), end with ROUTINE_OK. Surface only when a new coverage gap appeared — e.g. a new service without error tracking, an alert that's been silent too long, or an SLO that slipped. On the first run, always surface.", "schedule": "0 9 15 * *", "enabled": true, "suppress_when_silent": true, "created_at": "2026-04-24T00:00:00Z", "updated_at": "2026-04-24T00:00:00Z"}]
